title: What Is the Best Way to Own Gold in 2026?
author: Reuben Gregg Brewer
description: The article recommends that long-term investors seeking gold exposure should consider streaming and royalty companies like Franco-Nevada, Royal Gold, and Wheaton Precious Metals rather than direct gold ownership or mining stocks. These companies finance miners in exchange for future gold purchases at advantaged prices, offering diversified portfolios, growth potential, and protected margins without the operational risks of mining businesses.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Recommended as a superior option for long-term gold exposure due to diversified streaming/royalty portfolio, growth potential from securing new deals, and protected profit margins without mining operational risks.
Highlighted as a preferred streaming and royalty company offering gold exposure with diversification benefits and lower operational risk compared to direct mining.
Recommended alongside Franco-Nevada and Royal Gold as an attractive streaming/royalty company with differentiated business model and strong shareholder returns.
Presented as a viable but less optimal choice than streaming companies; while benefiting from high gold prices, mining operations are capital-intensive and concentrated in specific mines.

title: Canadian General Investments: Investment Update - Unaudited
author: Canadian General Investments, Limited
description: Canadian General Investments (CGI) reported a NAV of $86.82 per share at April 30, 2026, with year-to-date and 12-month NAV returns of 8.0% and 42.3% respectively, outperforming the S&P/TSX Composite Index. The company's leverage increased to 12.4% of net assets, and its portfolio is diversified across sectors with top holdings including Celestica, NVIDIA, and Franco-Nevada.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Largest holding in CGI's portfolio at 6.0%, indicating confidence in the company's prospects
Co-second-largest holding at 4.5%, indicating significant portfolio allocation to precious metals and mining sector

title: Discovery Produces 60,269 Ounces of Gold in First Quarter 2026
author: Discovery Silver Corp.
description: Discovery Silver Corp. announced Q1 2026 gold production of 60,269 ounces from its Porcupine operations, with guidance of 260,000-300,000 ounces for full year 2026. The company also announced the acquisition of Glencore's Kidd operations in Timmins, which includes processing infrastructure, tailings management, and exploration upside. Strong exploration results were reported across multiple operations.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Glencore is mentioned only as the seller of the Kidd operations. The transaction represents a divestment for Glencore but no sentiment-specific information about Glencore's operations or performance is provided in the article.
Franco-Nevada is mentioned only in a footnote regarding royalty arrangements related to gold ounces sold. No performance or sentiment-specific information is provided.

title: Why Smart Money is Securing Pivotal Gold Reserves Now
author: Usa News Group
description: Central banks are aggressively accumulating physical gold reserves while global mine output growth stalls at just 1%, creating a supply crunch that's driving institutional capital toward gold producers and reserve replacement players. Major gold companies are securing financing and advancing development projects as analysts project gold prices between $4,700-$5,400 per ounce in 2026, with deal values hitting 15-year peaks.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Maintains industry's largest gold reserve base with 118.2 million attributable ounces; holds 12.5 million tonnes of copper reserves and 442 million ounces of silver; budgeted $240 million exploration spending for 2026 with focus on near-mine expansion.

Delivered record 2025 results with $1.82 billion revenue (up 64% YoY), $1.49 billion operating cash flow, and $1.11 billion net income; announced 16% dividend increase; positioned with no debt and $3.1 billion available capital for continued shareholder value creation.

Filed NI 43-101 technical report for Island Gold District Expansion Study; operates among Canada's highest-grade underground gold operations; expansion study supports plans to significantly increase throughput and extend mine life.

Reported positive 2025 Back River Gold District exploration results with high-grade mineralization; committed $46 million to 2026 exploration program; drilling at Nuvuyak deposit extended mineralization 150m at depth with 700,000 ounces inferred resource.

title: Orezone Gold Completes Strategic Acquisition of Casa Berardi Gold Mine and Quebec Exploration Portfolio
author: Not Specified
description: Orezone Gold has completed its acquisition of Hecla Quebec Inc., gaining 100% ownership of the operating Casa Berardi gold mine and Quebec exploration properties. The transaction, valued at $352M upfront and deferred consideration plus up to $241M in contingent payments, positions Orezone as a diversified multi-asset producer. The company funded the acquisition through cash on hand and a gold stream agreement with Franco-Nevada, receiving a $100M upfront deposit. Orezone plans to optimize Casa Berardi operations and conduct an 80,000-100,000 metre annual exploration program.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Franco-Nevada is a financing partner providing a gold stream agreement with a $100M upfront deposit. The arrangement is structured as a commercial transaction with defined terms for gold deliveries and pricing, representing a neutral business relationship without indication of positive or negative implications.

Hecla Mining is the seller of the Casa Berardi mine and Quebec exploration portfolio. The transaction is presented as a strategic divestiture of non-core Canadian assets, with no indication of negative or positive sentiment regarding the sale itself.
